rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NICHOLAS HOVLAND 2015 SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/2021. Issues: 1) The account alleges the ending balance in First Republic Bank #4053 on 3/31/2020 was $133,425.71. The provided bank statement ends 03/31/2019. Please file bank statements that verify the balance at the end of the account period 3/31/2020. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES: 1) Petitioner has not filed a response to the following Examiner Notes: a. Objections allege notice was not given to Sharon So-Epprecht the alleged adult daughter of deceased sister Hazel So. Please explain why Sharon So-Epprecht is not listed on the petition page 4 under #8 and did not receive notice. Please explain if there are any other issue for Hazel So that must be listed on page 4 under #8. b. Please explain if there are any issue for Caroline Fong. c. Petitioner Paul Perdue did not give notice to Carolyn Espey as requested in request for special notice filed 05/12/2017. d. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 2) Petitioner Paul Perdue has not filed a response to the objections. 3) Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair alleged a supplemental I&A was filed showing cash of $2,039,727.47. No date for the filing was provided. No Supplemental I& has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CYNTHIA MAUREEN BELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/21. (No earlier availability.) No proof of service of notice of any of these hearings appears to have been filed. The register of actions does not reflect any, and no courtesy copies of any proof of service of a Notice of Hearing have ever been received by the Examiner. No copy of the "Declaration" filed on 10/26/20 has been received by the Examiner. The "Declaration (Third)," etc., filed on 2/24/21 AMENDED the prayer. The petition for final distribution filed on 4/24/20 specifically stated in paragraph 32 that the administrator was not requesting authorization to withhold a reserve. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] No written consent by the other heir has been filed consenting to the CHARITABLE GIFTS made out of the estate. The administrator's costs STILL DO NOT IDENTIFY THE ORIGINAL PAYEE for the payments attached to her creditor's claim. The attorney still has not set forth the specific costs to be reimbursed to her. Exhibit C attached to the "Second Declaration," etc., filed on 12/2/20, which was merely a printout of billing information, was not adequate, as noted in the previous tentative ruling. The date, payee, description (what was filed), and the amount must be clearly set forth. "Online service" or "read status" and the like will not be allowed. Courtesy copies of a proof of service of Notice of Hearing for the 4/27/21 hearing (or waiver of notice by the other heir and his attorney(s)), the "Declaration" filed on 10/26/20, and any other papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner by no later than 4/16/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL SALOMON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Ascertain Heirs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/21/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) The dates of death of all deceased ancestors through whom their issue claim entitlement to distribution must be specified. Even if exact dates cannot be ascertained, whether they died before or after the decedent must be specified. 2) The proof of service does not show notice was given to Garciano Aoanan's issue. 3) The proof of service does not show notice was given to Dario Aoanan, Marion Aoanan, Ester Aoanan, Teresta Fernandez, Magdalina Catalan, Erlinda Reyes, Simon Reyes, Rodolfo Reyes, Pedro Nario, Johnny Nario, Esmael Nario, and Imelda Abreo. No order prescribing or dispensing with notice has been issued for those without any known address. 4) Lorenzo Isla's address listed in the proof of service is different from the address listed in the amendment. 5) Villamor Solomon Isla's address listed in the proof of service is different from the address listed in the amendment. 6) Are all persons entitled to succeed to Rodrigo Solomon Isla's estate under the laws of his domicile at the time of his death listed in the amendment? Sufficient information concerning governing law must be provided. Notice must be given to all successors. The proof of service does not show notice was given to the 10 individuals associated with Rodrigo Solomon Isla and listed in the amendment. 7) Please provide an updated proposed order with the correct names of all persons entitled to distribution and their exact shares.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2021. 1) Should the request to increase the fees of the co-conservator of the person be deemed an amendment requiring new notice? Although the co-conservator's timesheets attached to the petition show the increased amount, neither paragraph 7 of the petition nor paragraph 5 of the prayer for relief show the increased amount or how the fees were calculated. 2) No statement or bank letter verifying the balance of the First Republic Bank account immediately preceding the date of appointment is on file. Probate Code section 2620(c)(2). 3) The First Republic Bank account statement includes a cancelled check of $578 to Chase Card Services on 04/13/2020, but according to Schedule C, the payee is SOMA Self-Storage.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALENTINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/07/2021 to be heard with the related petition for preliminary distribution. 1) Even though the current bond is insufficient, neither the petitioner nor her attorney has applied for an order increasing the bond. See CRC 7.204 requiring immediate application. 2) There is no guardian of the estate or guardian ad litem appointed for the minor beneficiary with authority to waive the bond. The guardian ad litem appointed in the related trust proceeding does not appear to have such authority. According to the order filed on 06/18/2019, the guardian ad litem is NOT authorized to waive any substantive rights of the minor beneficiary. The order approving the trust settlement filed on 02/18/2021 does not appear to provide the authority, either. Because the proposed preliminary distribution to the minor beneficiary cannot be made to his trust under Probate Code section 3413, the proposed preliminary distribution to the minor beneficiary must be included in calculating the bond increase.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALENTINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution, Settling First Account, Partial Allowance Of Administrators Commission And Costs And For Partial Allowance Of Ordinary Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/2021. 1) Please explain if the estate has received distribution from PTR-18-302398. If yes, this petition appears unnecessary and a petition for final distribution should be filed. If not please provide legal authority for the court determining preliminary distribution based on assets the estate has not received yet with a $240k liability. 2) Leland Valentine was appointed Administrator after a hearing with $300,000.00 bond. The I&A allege assets of $1,132,939.92. See LRSF 14.16 A. Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond and LRSF 14.11 B. Please provide legal authority for not applying for an increase in bond. 3) Please provide legal authority for the calculation of preliminary distribution not including the $240k liability to the trust. 4) Please provide legal authority for the calculation of attorney fees not including the $240k liability to the trust. 5) Please provide legal authority for distribution to Avid Eli Alavi as GAL for the benefit of David Valentine, Jr. Please identify the order appointing Avid Eli Alavi as GAL. Please provide legal authority for a GAL receiving distribution on behalf of a minor. 6) Administrator has kept assets over $250k limit in Schwab account. [Probate Code 9600]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF KAZUKO MIYAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/07/2021. 1) The real property has not been inventoried and appraised, nor has it been reported as an asset of the temporary conservatorship, even though the petitioner signed a trust transfer deed as grantor/conservator to transfer title to the trustee. 2) The petitioner's letters were never amended to include the additional powers granted by the Court's 09/03/2020 order. Probate Code section 2252(d). Were the trust, pour-over will, and trust transfer deed validly executed without amended letters? 3) No proof of recording letters is on file, although Exhibit B shows the paralegal's preparation of a recording cover sheet and payment of recording fees. 4) Did the petitioner investigate what happened to the temporary conservatee's Citibank account? 5) Why is the rental income reported as a lump sum on Schedule A? No information about monthly rent, number of rental units, property management fees, or security deposits as liabilities has been provided. Also, who was paying expenses to maintain the real property including taxes, insurance, and utilities during this account period? Schedule C does not show any payment. 6) Exhibits A & B mention "Wells Fargo account" and "Computershare". Are they assets of this temporary conservatorship? 7) Please provide a statement addressing CRC 7.702(1), (2), (3), & (4) factors in support of the fees requested by the petitioner's attorneys.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DECLARATION OF TRUST OF THE ROMELIA CHAVEZ TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Petition To Suspend And Remove Frances Vicky Gonzalez As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) To Charge Trustee'S Beneficial Interest; (3) For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/20/2021. ISSUES: 1) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902]. If there are contingent beneficiaries, please provide notice. [Probate Code 1202]. If any contingent beneficiaries are minors please provide legal authority for whether a Guardian Ad Litem needs to be appointed. 2) The Notice of Hearing shows notice being given 9/14/2020 prior to the petition being filed on 11/30/2020. Notice must be given after the petition is filed. Matter continued to provide proper 30 days' notice. (Probate Code 1202).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GIUSEPPE S. ORTENZO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/2021. Issues: 1) Please explain and file legal authority for entering TBD under all entries on page 2 #3.d or please file an amendment to include the estimated value of the property of the estate. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2) Petitioner did not check a required box on the petition page 3 under #5. A. (3) registered domestic partner or (4) no registered domestic partner. 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Please explain why there are two dates on Francesco Ortenzo's waiver of bond. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
